blockers.description: Installed oauth_helper.exe exits 2: 'DLL load failed while importing QtWebEngineWidgets: The specified procedure could not be found.' Helper bundle is COMPLETE (QtWebEngineWidgets.pyd + QtWebEngineCore.pyd + Qt6WebEngineCore.dll + Qt6WebEngineWidgets.dll + Qt6WebEngineQuick.dll + QtWebEngineProcess.exe all present). Strong hypothesis: conda Library\bin Qt6Core.dll on PATH shadows the helper's bundled pip Qt (the exit=2 reproductions were run FROM the (musicstreamer-build) conda shell).
blockers.workaround: Decisive test pending: run helper with sanitized PATH (System32 only) or from a clean non-conda shell. Also grep global PATH for conda — if conda is on the GLOBAL PATH, even the Start-Menu app inherits it and the helper needs PATH-hardening at startup (real 88.3-04 fix).

human_actions_pending.action: Run sanitized-PATH test of oauth_helper.exe on the VM
Check whether conda is on the GLOBAL PATH
human_actions_pending.context: In a fresh PowerShell (no conda activate): `$env:PATH -split ';' | Select-String 'conda|miniforge|miniconda|Library\\bin'`. Empty => Start-Menu app launches clean. Non-empty => helper needs PATH-hardening.

human_actions_pending.action: Run the three in-app logins from the INSTALLED build (GBS.FM, Twitch, Google/YouTube) and record pass/issue in 88.3-UAT.md
human_actions_pending.context: G6-T6/T7/T8 close-out: WebEngine window opens, no exit=2, no DLL-load failure; Twitch accepted (UA fix); GBS cookie-fallback does NOT auto-trigger.

decisions.decision: B1 isolated-helper architecture is correct and validated
decisions.rationale: Mixing pip WebEngine into the conda env breaks QtCore (ICU ABI clash) — proven this session by the contamination failure; WebEngine must live in the separate oauth_helper.exe

decisions.decision: conda musicstreamer-build env must stay pip-PySide6-free
decisions.rationale: Leftover pip PySide6-Essentials/Addons from the pre-B1 attempt caused 'specified procedure could not be found' on QtCore import; fix = pip uninstall + conda force-reinstall. Saved to project memory build-env-must-stay-pip-pyside6-free

decisions.decision: Local pytest must use .venv/bin/python and be scoped to phase-relevant tests
decisions.rationale: System python3 lacks PySide6 (55 false collection errors); the full Qt GUI suite times out >540s. Run packaging-spec + oauth tests with .venv/bin/python

next_action: On the VM, run the sanitized-PATH test of oauth_helper.exe (blocker #1). If it opens the GBS window -> confirmed PATH shadowing: check global PATH for conda; if clean, launch the installed app from the Start Menu (not the conda shell) and run the 3 logins. If sanitized-PATH still fails, or conda is on global PATH, the helper needs startup PATH-hardening in oauth_helper_standalone.spec / oauth_helper.py (88.3-04 gap-closure fix) then rebuild. Then record results in 88.3-UAT.md and resume /gsd:execute-phase 88.3 for verification + completion.
context_notes: Phase 88.3 Waves 1-2 are COMPLETE and merged on main (do NOT re-execute plans 01-04). Only 88.3-05 (the human-gated Win11 VM UAT) remains. The current failure is NOT a code regression — it is an environment/bundle DLL-resolution problem surfaced by the UAT (which is exactly its job). Two PATH-shadowing failures already diagnosed this session: (1) conda env pip-PySide6 contamination broke the MAIN bundle's QtCore — FIXED via env cleanup; (2) the HELPER bundle now exits 2 on QtWebEngineWidgets import, same 'procedure could not be found' family, hypothesized as conda Qt on PATH shadowing the helper's pip Qt because the exe was run from the conda shell. The spike's 'Stage C PASS — no PATH mitigation needed' was validated in a CLEAN env, consistent with this being a launched-from-conda-shell artifact rather than a true bundle defect. Resume by getting the sanitized-PATH test result first.
